etymology.description: 屮(屮) ('vegetation') + 田(田) ('field') = garden -> corrupted into 父(父) ('father') + 用(用) ('to use') -> phono-semantic: 父 for sound
etymology.interpretation: Originally, 甫 depicted a piece of land or garden through the combination of vegetation (屮) and a field (田). Over time, the components evolved in script, with 父 becoming the phonetic element, contributing the sound. While originally referring to gardens, its phonetic role led to its modern use related to men or gentlemen.

examples.chinese: 这个小村庄只有一个男子甫(這個小村莊只有一個男子甫)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège xiǎo cūnzhuāng zhǐyǒu yīgè nánzǐ fǔ.
examples.english: This small village has only one man named Fu.

examples.chinese: 甫(甫)是一位出色的画家(是一位出色的畫家)。
examples.pinyin: Fǔ shì yī wèi chūsè de huàjiā.

examples.chinese: 他们在讨论甫的生平(他們在討論甫的生平)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài tǎolùn fǔ de shēngpíng.
